text:
Olympic organisers have made the decision to cancel a second consecutive day of triathlon training due to concerns over pollution in the Seine.

The triathlon event is one of the most physically demanding events at the Olympics with its swimming section scheduled to take place in the iconic river.

However, on Sunday morning it was pushed back due to concerns over the quality of the water.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for latest news on the Paris Olympics

Officials confirmed in the early hours of Monday morning that training for the event has been cancelled for the second day in a row due to those same fears – but remain confident the event will go ahead as planned on Tuesday.

In a joint statement, Paris 2024 organisers and World Triathlon said they had taken the decision to cancel Monday’s swimming training session because ‘water quality levels… do not present sufficient guarantees’ to allow it to take place.

‘Given the weather forecast for the next 36 hours, Paris 2024 and World Triathlon are confident that water quality will return to below limits before the start of the triathlon competitions on July 30,’ organisers added.

The opening day of the Olympics was hit by adverse weather conditionswhich led to the postponement of the skateboardingwith tennis also hit by delays.

55 triathletes are scheduled to line up at 6am GMT on Tuesday and dive into the Siene, marking the first time athletes have competed in the river at a Olympics since 1900.

France has invested some $1.4 billion in new wastewater infrastructure to cut the amounts of sewage flowing into the river, and city authorities have announced plans for three swimming sites to open to the public by June next year.

Paris Mayor Anne Hidalgo and Paris 2024 chief organiser Tony Estanguet swam in the Seine on July 17 to demonstrate the water was clean but the heavy rainfall has dramatically altered the situation.

Tests as recently as June still showed traces of E.coli in the river with the quality of the water still a major concern heading into the Games.

The swimming events that’ll take place in the Seine River include marathon swimming and triathlon.

Other swimming events, including Adam Peaty’s attempts to bring home a medal later today (July 28), will take place at the La Défense Arena.

title: Olympic event cancelled for second day in a row due to River Seine concerns | Metro News
description: Paris Olympics organisers have cancelled a second consecutive day of triathlon training due to concerns over pollution in the River Seine.
